editorNote: A curated view of 10 stories shaping today's Hacker News conversation.

stories.headline: Kaiser nurses say AI, surveillance are making their jobs and patient care worse
stories.summary: Kaiser Permanente advice nurse Raquel Alvarez Sanchez works from her home office in Santa Rosa on April 6, 2026.  Kaiser Permanente nurses have raised concerns about the growing use of AI to monitor their work ahead of upcoming contract negotiations.
stories.whyItMatters: The Hacker News community gave this story 495 points.

stories.headline: First atmosphere found on Earth-like planet in habitable zone of distant star
stories.summary: First atmosphere found on Earth-like planet in habitable zone of distant star 20 hours ago Share Save Add as preferred on Google Pallab Ghosh Science Correspondent Melissa Weiss/Center for Astrophysics |Harvard & Smithsonian Artwork: The red tinge illustrates an atmosphere around a rocky planet orbting a red star Researchers have found the first atmosphere surrounding an Earth-like, rocky planet orbiting within the habitable zone of a distant star.  The researchers say that their discovery provides the strongest evidence yet that worlds with conditions similar to Earth could exist beyond our solar system.
stories.whyItMatters: The Hacker News community gave this story 447 points.

stories.headline: The state of open source AI
stories.summary: A Letter From Our CTO, Raffi Krikorian “ In New Zealand's far north, a Māori broadcaster trains speech models for te reo — a language too small for any market — under a license that keeps the data with its people.  PwC, one of the largest accounting firms in the world, fine-tuned an open model on the language of finance and runs it today for hundreds of clients, on its own hardware, with no per-token meter running.
stories.whyItMatters: The Hacker News community gave this story 431 points.

stories.headline: Regressive JPEGs
stories.summary: Regressive JPEGs: 2026-07-17 One of the cool features of JPEG files is that there's the option to save low frequency components first.  This means that a partially downloaded image will be displayed at low resolution instead of being cut off.
stories.whyItMatters: The Hacker News community gave this story 270 points.

stories.headline: The Zilog Z80 has turned 50
stories.summary: The Zilog Z80 has turned 50 Back to the Blog Index Introduction As of writing, the Zilog Z80 processor was officially launched 50 years ago, in July of 1976, less than 4 years after the last human had walked on the moon, decades closer to WWII than to the present day, roughly at a half way point between the Kennedy assassination and the fall of the Berlin wall, closer to the Korean war than to 9/11 which is itself an event that happened a quarter of a century ago.  (Sorry…) The processor was extremely successful, being used in many 8 bit microcomputers, including early personal computers, home & hobby computers, as well as many embedded, industrial applications.
stories.whyItMatters: The Hacker News community gave this story 215 points.
